summary: Iteration 3 re-review of the Dashboard UI against guides/backend-guide.md and guides/ui-style-guide.md. The core architecture is strongly aligned: business logic lives in query objects (app/queries/dashboard/*) and a Dashboard::Broadcast service; HomeController and PhasesController are thin and branch only on result.success?/response format; broadcasts fire from services after commit and target the smallest DOM unit via a per-user stream (BroadcastCard defaults dashboard:true, progress ticks opt out with dashboard:false); the shared status_badge helper carries semantic tones with the status word always shown; the presentation-boundary rescue is scoped to exactly one read per panel; and Minitest covers queries, service, controller, and system flows. The two genuinely new patterns (presentation-boundary rescue and the cross-pipeline per-user stream) were added to both guides in the same PR, exactly as CLAUDE.md requires. No blockers or majors. The same five minor deviations from iteration 2 were re-verified against the current code and remain unaddressed; each is trivially fixable and none disturbs the sound architecture.

findings.issue: Off-scale semantic status colors. ui-style-guide.md > Color reserves `red-600` for stuck/failed/blocked and `green-600` for success/converged. The attention border uses `border-red-500` (line 10) and the completed-phase progress segments use `bg-green-500` (line 29). The `border-amber-500` and `bg-indigo-600` on the same lines are correct; only the red/green fills drift to the -500 step. No other view uses solid -500 status fills, so this is newly introduced palette drift. Fix: `border-red-600` / `bg-green-600`. Severity: minor.

Hand-rolled badge bypasses the single-source component. ui-style-guide.md > Core components requires 'one source of truth per component', and the shared status_badge helper (app/helpers/status_helper.rb) already produces the soft pill for both tones and accepts a `label:`. The attention pill instead inlines the full class string (bg-amber-50 text-amber-700 ring-1 ring-amber-600/20, and the red equivalent). status_badge("awaiting_human", label: "Needs your input") / status_badge("stuck", label: "Stuck") yield the same pill (the only extras are gap-1 and a decorative ● dot); note the inline copy also omits the helper's ring-inset — exactly the drift a shared component prevents. Severity: minor.

Business logic in a view helper rather than a reusable PORO. backend-guide.md > 'Business logic lives in reusable POROs' says such logic belongs in plain Ruby objects callable from controllers, jobs, the console, and tests — not a helper mixed into views. define_open_questions_structured / latest_structured_questions_run select the authoritative StepRun across phases->workflows->step_runs (flat_map + max_by on iteration/attempt/id) and JSON-parse the artifact payload, and duplicate the near-identical run-selection already in latest_open_questions_run. This belongs in a query/domain object with the helper reduced to a thin call. Severity: minor.

Empty states omit the primary action the guide mandates. ui-style-guide.md > Core components: 'Empty states: icon + one sentence + primary action. Never a bare empty table.' The 'No projects yet' state in index.html.erb correctly includes a primary action, but 'No active pipelines', 'No recent activity', and 'No workers connected' provide only text — an inconsistency within the same PR. (The workers case is weakest, since worker registration is a CLI action with no UI equivalent; a link to Workers or Projects would still satisfy the rule.) Severity: minor.

Fleet panel is kept current by fixed-interval client polling (turbo_frame_tag ... src: + poll_frame_controller.js reloading every 30s) rather than Turbo Stream broadcasts. ui-style-guide.md > Principles #3 ('Live by default … updates in place via Turbo Streams') and Real-time behavior push for stream-driven updates on worker state. This is a deliberate, documented deviation (worker heartbeats don't broadcast to the dashboard and per-heartbeat fan-out would storm) and it does avoid a manual refresh, so it is borderline — but it departs from the stated stream-first convention and, unlike the two sanctioned patterns, neither guide was amended to allow the poll fallback. Worth an explicit manager decision or a one-line guide note. Severity: minor.
